briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. We will now begin the climate briefing for July 1, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Global Climate Crisis: Warning Bells for Daily Life and Industry
sections.content: Climate-related news released today clearly demonstrates that the global climate crisis is no longer a distant future concern. Domestically, the potential for a crisis in coffee production has been reported [1], and in the waters off Jeju, the appearance of endangered whale sharks and the mass collapse of soft coral colonies have been identified as impacts of climate change [3, 8, 15]. Internationally, global sea surface temperatures in June reached an all-time high due to the combined effects of El Niño and climate change [177, 188]. Analysis also suggests that extreme heatwaves across Europe and the United States are being further exacerbated by climate change [171, 173, 174]. Warnings have also been raised that these climate changes are shaking the financial system itself and increasing the list of uninsurable assets [183, 186], once again highlighting the urgent need for a widespread response across our entire society.

sections.title: 🌱 Continued Domestic and International Efforts for Carbon Neutrality
sections.content: Despite the warnings of a climate crisis, various efforts to achieve carbon neutrality are actively continuing both domestically and internationally. Law firm Barun and the Climate Change Center signed a business agreement to strengthen legal and policy support for climate crisis response and the expansion of ESG management [2, 6, 7, 9, 10, 12, 14, 17]. Major corporations such as SKC, L&F, Korea Zinc, and LG Uplus are accelerating ESG management by publishing sustainability reports and detailing their carbon neutrality roadmaps [11, 35, 47, 50, 54, 58, 64, 68, 69, 73, 76, 86, 87, 100]. Local governments are also implementing practical community-centered policies, including Yongin City's climate crisis adaptation policy receiving the highest national evaluation for two consecutive years [18, 28], Taebaek City's presentation of a clean energy carbon-neutral city vision [30, 59, 96, 97], and Yeonggwang County and Yeosu City providing incentives for carbon neutrality practices [48, 93].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Current Challenges
sections.content: In the domestic renewable energy sector, large-scale investments are continuing, with the SK Group partnering with global investment firm KKR to launch Korea's largest integrated new and renewable energy corporation, valued at 2 trillion won [101, 103, 108, 110, 112, 119, 123, 125, 129, 137, 140, 150, 152, 159, 160]. President Lee Jae-myung's site visit to renewable energy facilities in the Southwest region [131, 167] and former President Moon Jae-in's remarks on fostering a renewable energy base [109, 120, 126, 145] demonstrate a strong government commitment. However, paradoxical situations, such as the domestic module industry facing a crisis due to a 'domestic cell-first' policy [104, 106], and the increase in greenhouse gas emissions despite Samsung Electro-Mechanics' increased use of renewable energy [111], suggest the complexity of the transition process. Furthermore, challenges like securing power and the cost burden for 'power-intensive industries' known as megaprojects [154], and the recognition, as seen in Switzerland's case, that solar power alone cannot meet winter electricity demand [158], emphasize the importance of a stable energy mix and technological innovation in expanding renewable energy.

sections.title: 🌍 Climate Change, Geopolitical Power Reshaping, and the Role of Industry
sections.content: Climate change is emerging as a factor reshaping global geopolitical power, extending beyond mere environmental concerns [4, 25]. Amidst these changes, Manfred Weber of Europe expressed the stance that 'we cannot kill our industries because of climate change' [172, 175, 178, 185], illustrating that finding a balance between climate policy and industrial competitiveness is becoming a key issue. Meanwhile, a positive outlook has also been presented, suggesting that the construction industry can be part of the solution to climate change [187], which implies the need for each industrial sector to actively participate in addressing the climate crisis and seek innovative solutions. We must deepen our understanding of this complex issue through diverse perspectives and discussions, such as attempts to redefine climate change as 'climate reversal' [182].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing clearly illustrated the reality of the climate crisis, the comprehensive efforts being made to address it, and the challenges encountered during this transition. We must continue to foster a climate-resilient future through sustained interest and cooperation. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: 기아 6월 판매 호조로 상반기 신기록…현대차는 ‘주춤’
articles.description: 스포티지 하이브리드. 기아 제공현대자동차와 기아의 지난달 국내외 판매 실적이 크게 엇갈렸다. 기아는 신차 효과와 친환경차 인기에 힘입어 창사 이래 최대 상반기 실적을 올린 반면, 현대차는 내수와 해외 시장이 동시에 위축되며 주춤한 모습을 보였다.기아는 올해 상반기(1~6월) 글로벌 시장에서 국내 29만5779대, 해외 133만2473대 등 총 163만988···

summary.phenomenon: 기아는 올해 상반기(1~6월) 글로벌 시장에서 총 163만988대(국내 29만5779대, 해외 133만2473대)를 판매하며 창사 이래 최대 상반기 실적을 기록했다. 반면 현대차는 내수와 해외 시장이 동시에 위축되며 주춤한 모습을 보였다.
summary.cause: 기아의 판매 호조는 신차 효과와 친환경차 인기에 힘입은 것으로 분석된다.

articles.title: 호르무즈 위기 최대 수혜자는 중국···에너지 비축·재생에너지로 충격 흡수, 반사이익까지
articles.description: 중국 서북부 칭하이성 일대 사막의 태양광 단지. 2024년 10월 촬영. 신화연합뉴스미국과 이란의 전쟁으로 촉발된 호르무즈 해협 위기에서 중국이 최대 수혜자가 됐다는 분석이 나왔다.영국 일간 가디언은 30일(현지시간) 미국 지정학 컨설팅 기업 아시아그룹이 발표한 보고서를 인용해 중국이 중동 원자재 위기의 충격을 흡수하는 데 성공했을 뿐 아니라 전쟁이 촉발한···

summary.phenomenon: 2024년 10월 촬영된 칭하이성 일대 사막의 태양광 단지 사진과 함께, 미국과 이란의 전쟁으로 촉발된 호르무즈 해협 위기에서 중국이 최대 수혜자가 되었다는 분석이 나왔다.
summary.cause: 중국은 중동 원자재 위기의 충격을 에너지 비축과 재생에너지 확대를 통해 흡수하는 데 성공했으며, 전쟁으로 인한 반사이익까지 얻었다.
